concerns.text: Plan mandates p-values and confidence intervals (FR-008, Constitution VII) for correlations on a finite census of prime knots (≤13). Statistical inference (p-values) implies sampling from a larger population, which does not exist for a complete enumeration of mathematical objects. This renders inferential claims mathematically unsound for the stated scope. Results should be framed as descriptive model fit (R², MAE) rather than statistical significance.

research.md states 'NO verified URL exists for Knot Atlas' and advises describing by name only, yet spec.md FR-001 mandates download from 'https://katlas.org'. This contradiction undermines Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility). If the URL is unstable, the 'Single Source of Truth' cannot be verified. Resolve data source stability or switch to a verified, checksummed mirror.

Validation via 'exploratory validation sample' (random split) on a finite census risks overfitting to the specific subset. Since the dataset is the population (knots ≤13), train/test splits are for model comparison only, not generalization. Clarify that 'validation' refers to model selection, not statistical prediction accuracy on unseen data.
